findings.summary: Olimex ESP32-PRO development board with ESP32-D0WDQ6, PIC32MX270F256D co-processor, MCP73833 LiPo charger, SY8089 buck converter, SY6280 USB power switch, LY68S3200SLT PSRAM, MX25U3235F SPI flash, ATECC508A crypto, and micro SD. Analyzer produced strong results on crystal circuits, decoupling, I2C, and memory interfaces.

correct.description: Three crystal circuits correctly detected: Q1 (8.0MHz/20pF) with C13/C14 (33pF) giving 19.5pF effective load, Q2 (32.768KHz/12.5pF) with C15/C16 (33pF), and Q3 (40.000MHz/12pF) with C20/C21 (22pF) giving 14pF effective load
D2 (SMBJ6.0A) correctly detected as TVS protection diode on +5V rail clamping to GND
D1 (1N5822/SS34) correctly detected as reverse polarity protection between +5V_EXT and +5V
I2C bus correctly detected on SCL (RB8/SCL1) and SDA (RB9/SDA1) nets with pullups R42/R43 to +3.3V, devices U6 (ATECC508A), U1 (PIC32MX), U5 (ESP32)
Memory interface U8 (LY68S3200SLT PSRAM) correctly detected with 3 shared signals to U5 (ESP32)
Memory interface U7 (MX25U3235F flash) correctly detected connected to U5 (ESP32)
Decoupling analysis: +5V rail with 3x 47uF (141uF total), +3.3V with 12 caps (49.8uF), +1.8V with 3 caps (1.2uF) all correctly tallied
LC filter L5 (2.4nH)/C30 (4.7pF) resonating at 1.50 GHz correctly detected - this is the ESP32 2.4GHz antenna matching network
RC filter R1/C1 at 159 Hz and R2/C1+C17 at 723 Hz correctly detected on MCLR reset circuit
Voltage divider R23/R22 (4.99k/10k) at ratio 0.667 feeding USB-OTG fault detection pin correctly detected
131 components, 8 ICs, 47 resistors, 41 caps, 6 inductors, 3 crystals - all counts match the schematic
6 fiducials correctly classified as fiducial type

incorrect.description: U3 (SY8089AAAC) switching regulator detected with input_rail=GND which is wrong. The SY8089 input comes from +5V rail. The analyzer may be confusing the GND pin with the VIN pin
U3 (SY8089) regulator is missing output_rail and inductor detection. The SY8089 LX pin connects to the inductor and the output should be +3.3V

Voltage divider R18/R19 (4.99k/1.1k) mid_point connected to U3 pin 3 (LX). This is actually the feedback divider for the SY8089 buck converter (FB pin, not LX), suggesting pin mapping is incorrect for this part

Voltage divider R10/R11 (1.1k/10k) at ratio 0.90 with mid_point connection to THERM1 is detected as a voltage divider. While technically a divider, this is a thermistor circuit for battery temperature sensing, not a signal divider
Regulator caps observation says missing input cap on GND for U3 - this is a consequence of the incorrect input_rail=GND detection

missed.description: MCP73833 (U2) LiPo battery charger IC not detected in bms_systems. It manages battery charging with STAT1/STAT2 outputs and PG (power good) indicator
SY6280AAC (U4) USB power switch not detected. It controls +5V_USB-OTG power with overcurrent/fault protection - this is a notable power management component
ESP32 antenna matching network (L5/C30 LC detected) should also include the nearby components L4, C29, C28 which form the complete RF matching network to ANT1

suggestions: Fix SY8089/SY8009 pin mapping - the FB and LX pins appear swapped, causing incorrect regulator detection
Add SY6280 and similar USB power switches to protection_devices or a new power_switch category

findings.summary: The analyzer correctly identified the major ICs (ESP32, PIC32, MCP73833, SY8089, SY6280, ATECC508A, MX25U3235F, LY68S3200SLT), three crystal circuits with accurate load cap calculations, the R18/R19 voltage divider for the buck converter feedback, I2C bus with pullups, decoupling analysis across four power rails, and protection devices D2 (TVS) and D1 (reverse polarity). However, it has significant issues with net tracing: U3 input_rail is null (should be +5V via battery/FET path), the output_rail is __unnamed_4 instead of +3.3V, the battery charger U2 was not detected in battery_chargers, and the U4 load switch power_path entry has incorrect input_rail/output_rail. The voltage divider R18/R19 midpoint incorrectly shows connection to U3 pin 3 (LX) instead of pin 5 (FB). Several pin_nets are missing or incorrect due to wire tracing issues in the legacy .sch format.

correct.description: Three crystal circuits correctly identified: Q1 (8MHz/PIC32, CL_eff=19.5pF vs 20pF spec), Q2 (32.768kHz/PIC32, CL_eff=19.5pF vs 12.5pF spec), Q3 (40MHz/ESP32, CL_eff=14pF vs 12pF spec) with correct load cap refs
R18 (4.99k) / R19 (1.1k) correctly identified as voltage divider with ratio 0.1806 and nets +3.3V top / GND bottom
D2 (SMBJ6.0A) correctly identified as TVS protection on +5V rail clamping to GND
D1 (SS34) correctly identified as reverse_polarity protection between +5V_EXT and +5V
I2C bus on SCL/SDA nets correctly detected with U6, U1, U5 as devices, R42/R43 (2.2k) pullups to +3.3V
Decoupling analysis correctly identifies 4 rails: +5V (141uF/3 caps), +3.3V (49.8uF/12 caps), +5V_USB-OTG (0.1uF/1 cap), +1.8V (1.2uF/3 caps)
LED audit correctly identifies PWRLED1, COMPLETE1, CHARGING1, PWR_GOOD1 with correct series resistors (all 2.2k) and supply net +5V
Memory interfaces U8 (PSRAM) and U7 (flash) correctly detected with ESP32 U5 as connected processor
LC filter L5 (2.4nH) / C30 (4.7pF) correctly identified at 1.50 GHz resonance - this is the ESP32 antenna matching network
R10 (1.1k) / R11 (10k) voltage divider correctly identified - this is the MCP73833 THERM thermistor voltage divider with THERM1 test pad at midpoint
Component counts accurate: 131 total, 8 ICs, 47 resistors, 41 capacitors, 3 crystals, 4 LEDs, 6 connectors, 4 ferrite beads
U5 (ESP32) power domains correctly identified as +1.8V and +3.3V
RC filter R21 (10k) / C32 (100nF) at 159.15 Hz correctly identified as ESP32_EN reset RC delay circuit
ESD coverage audit correctly flags USB-OTG1 as high_risk with no ESD protection on D+/D-/ID lines

incorrect.description: U3 (SY8089) power_regulator input_rail is null - should be the battery/5V input rail. The schematic shows U3 pin 4 (IN) connected to the battery charger output path via FET2 and D3
U3 (SY8089) output_rail shows __unnamed_4 instead of +3.3V. The inductor L2 output connects to the +3.3V power symbol
Voltage divider R18/R19 midpoint shows connection to U3 pin 3 (LX) which is the switching node output, not the feedback pin. The midpoint should connect to U3 pin 5 (FB)

U4 (SY6280AAC) power_path entry has input_rail='RB14/USB-OTG_EN(VBUSON)' and output_rail='GND'. The SY6280 IN connects to +5V, OUT goes to +5V_USB-OTG, EN is controlled by RB14. The analyzer confused the enable pin with the input rail
U2 (MCP73833) pin_nets show pin 2 (VDD2) as GND and pin 5 (VSS) as +5V - these are swapped
U3 (SY8089) pin_nets show only pin 4 (IN) connected to GND, but this is wrong. Pin 2 is GND, Pin 4 (IN) should connect to the input voltage rail
U4 pin_nets show pin 1 (OUT) as GND and pin 2 (GND) as +5V - these are swapped
U1 (PIC32) power_domains includes RB3, RC0, RC1, RC2 as power_rails - these are GPIO signal pins, not power rails
U8 (PSRAM) power_domains includes 'ESP32_GPIO7/SD_DATA0' as a power rail - this is a data signal, not a power rail
R23/R22 voltage divider: this is really a pull-up/pull-down resistor network on a USB-OTG FAULT status signal, not a classical voltage divider

missed.description: U2 (MCP73833) LiPo battery charger IC not detected in battery_chargers. It manages battery charging with STAT1/STAT2 outputs and PG indicator, PROG pin R6=2.2k sets charge current to ~455mA
Power path management circuit with FET1 and FET2 (WPM2015 P-MOSFETs) not detected. FET1 provides power OR-ing between +5V_EXT and +5V_USB-OTG. FET2 manages battery/charger output to buck converter U3
SD/MMC card interface not detected as a bus protocol. MICRO_SD1 connector connects via series resistors to ESP32 HS2_xxx SDIO pins with pullups R27-R30
USB-OTG interface not detected. USB-OTG1 micro-USB connector with D+/D- through ferrite L3 to PIC32 USB pins, ID pin to RB5, VBUS through load switch U4
ESP32 auto-programming circuit with D4 (BAT54C) and transistor logic using DTR/RTS to control ESP32_EN and GPIO0 for boot mode selection
1.8V power domain for flash and PSRAM: R30 (2.2k) pulls GPIO12 to +3.3V which configures ESP32 internal LDO to output 1.8V on VDD_SDIO
UART bridge between PIC32 (U1) and ESP32 (U5) not detected. RC7/U1TXD and RC6/U1RXD connect to ESP32 GPIO3/U0RXD and GPIO1/U0TXD through R33, R34 (220R)
SPI bus between PIC32 and ESP32 not detected. Shared VSPI signals: GPIO18/VSPICLK, GPIO19/VSPIQ, GPIO23/VSPID, GPIO5/VSPICS0

suggestions: Fix legacy .sch wire tracing for U3 - pin_nets are mostly empty or wrong, causing cascading failures in regulator detection
Add battery charger detection for MCP73833 and similar LiPo charger ICs
Detect P-MOSFET power path circuits used for power OR-ing and reverse polarity protection
Detect SDIO/SD card interfaces by matching HS2_xxx pin name pattern and MICRO_SD connector
U4 power_path detection should identify IN pin as input_rail and OUT pin as output_rail, not confuse EN pin with input_rail
Power domain detection for U1 incorrectly includes GPIO signals as power rails - filter by pin type
ESP32 auto-programming circuit (DTR/RTS via BAT54C to EN/GPIO0) is a common pattern that should be detected
